title: Functions
text: Functions The ProcScript programming language provides many functions to access or manipulate data or return status information. For example, ProcScript functions can be used to: Request certain values (status request functions) Set or reset values ( $instancemod , $fieldcheck , $occcheck , $batch , $hide , $disable , and $check ) Inspect a value ( $previous and $next ) Convert data from one format to another ( $number , $syntax , $date , $datim , and $clock ) Set application shell variables ( $keyboard , $variation , and $language ) Return and set values of Uniface status and result codes ( $status and $procerror ) Set the cursor ( $prompt ) The return value of a function can be directly assigned to a field or variable. The general format of a ProcScript function is: Function { ( Argument 1 , Argument n ) } where: Function —specifies the literal name of a Uniface ProcScript function (which always begins with $ ) Argument —specifies an expression of which the result is passed as argument to function. For example, string arguments can be a literal string, or field, variable, function, or parameter that evaluates to a string. For more information, see ProcScript: Functions . Examples of ProcScript Functions vEntity = $entname ; name of the current entity vField = $fieldname ; name of the current field TOTAL = $hits(DOCUMENTS) ; size of the hitlist for entity DOCUMENTS CURRENT_TIME = $clock ; current time Using Functions in Expressions A ProcScript function can be used as an operand in an expression or as an argument in another function. This mechanism is often used in global ProcScript so that the ProcScript is not tied to a particular field name. For example: $1 = $number($fieldname) Copy Assigning Values to Functions Some functions can have a value assigned to them. For example: $formtitle = "This is my form" Copy This mechanism is used to assign the language and library for an application shell. For example, the apStart trigger often contains statements similar to these: $language = "USA" $variation = "TECH_PUBS" Copy Setting Logical Values The ProcScript instructions set and reset can be used with some functions that take logical values. For example: set $check Copy Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
